model_card.name: Potkay2021_S10_SimulationDescriptions_Protocols
task: Specify the simulation experiment designs (control, drought/groundwater, competition/light, elevated CO2, pruning recovery) used to evaluate THORP’s biomass partitioning predictions and sensitivities
Define forcing datasets, initialization conditions, run duration/time step, and treatment perturbations for each experiment

assumptions.text: S.10 provides detailed descriptions of the ten simulations summarized in Table 2 of the main text; most results are shown in main-text Figures 2–6, and pruning experiments in Figure S11.

evidence.snippet: 52    S.10 SIMULATION DESCRIPTIONS    This section describes in full detail the ten simulations summarized in Table 2 in the main text.  Results are presented in the main text (Figures 2-6), except for those experiments with a pruning, which are in Figure S11.

assumptions.text: When reporting biomass fractions, dry biomass is assumed 50% carbon by weight, except roots assumed 55% carbon by weight.

evidence.snippet: … THORP simulates the size of carbon pools and not  explicitly biomass; when biomass fractions are reported, they were calculated assuming the dry biomass  is 50% carbon by weight, except roots which were assumed to be 55% carbon by weight (Janssens et al.,  1999).

variables.name: leaf mass fraction
variables.definition: Leaf biomass fraction used for reporting partitioning vs total dry mass.

evidence.snippet: … biomass fractions (leaf mass fraction, LMF; aboveground woody (stem) mass fraction, SMF; root mass fraction, RMF) …

variables.name: aboveground woody (stem) mass fraction
variables.definition: Aboveground woody biomass fraction used for reporting partitioning.

evidence.snippet: … biomass fractions (leaf mass fraction, LMF; aboveground woody (stem) mass fraction, SMF; root mass fraction, RMF) …

variables.name: root mass fraction
variables.definition: Root biomass fraction used for reporting partitioning.

evidence.snippet: … biomass fractions (leaf mass fraction, LMF; aboveground woody (stem) mass fraction, SMF; root mass fraction, RMF) …

variables.name: rooting depth (95%)
variables.definition: Depth above which 95% of root biomass is present; used as rooting depth metric in figures.

evidence.snippet: … Rooting depth is generally reported as the depth above which 95% of root biomass is present (Z95% in Figure 2a,c,  5a,b,c, S.6a,e, S.7a,e, S.8a,e, S.9a,e) …

variables.name: rooting depth (99.5%)
variables.definition: Depth above which 99.5% of root biomass is present; used in some analyses (e.g., Figure 5c).

evidence.snippet: … and we sometimes consider the depth above which 99.5% of root  biomass is present (Z99.5% in Figure 5c).

variables.name: sapwood-to-leaf area ratio
variables.definition: Ratio of sapwood and leaf areas, reported to compare treatments to control.

evidence.snippet: … compare biomass partitioning (LMF, SMF, RMF), rooting  depth, and Huber values (the ratio of sapwood and leaf areas) …

parameters.name: simulation time step
parameters.notes: Control experiment run at 6-hour time steps.

evidence.snippet: … The model was simulated for a total of 100-years  at 6-hour time-steps with NOAA re-analysis data (NCEP/NCAR Reanalysis 1; Kalnay et al., 1996) as  environmental forcing …

parameters.name: atmospheric CO2 concentration (elevated treatment)
parameters.notes: Elevated-CO2 experiment uses 600 ppm; nutrient limitation not represented in current THORP version.

evidence.snippet: … identical to  the control except CO2 concentrations were elevated to 600 ppm … The current version of THORP does not  describe nutrient acquisition and partitioning …

parameters.name: overstory canopy height (competition treatment)
parameters.notes: Overstory canopy introduced after spin-up; LAI=3.

evidence.snippet: … except a 30 m tall overstory with a LAI of 3 was  introduced … after the ~2.6 spin-up  period.

parameters.name: overstory leaf area index (competition treatment)
parameters.notes: Overstory canopy LAI in shading experiment.

evidence.snippet: … except a 30 m tall overstory with a LAI of 3 was  introduced …

parameters.name: overstory canopy extinction coefficient
parameters.notes: Used in S.5 for shading experiment (replacing Table 3 value).

evidence.snippet: … (Κn = 3.2 ∙ 10-2 m-1 in S.5 of SI instead of value in Table 3 in main text) …

… 6-hour time-steps with NOAA re-analysis data (NCEP/NCAR Reanalysis 1; Kalnay et al., 1996) as  environmental forcing, namely air and soil temperatures, relative air humidity of the air, precipitation,  windspeeds, and incoming solar insolation. … relative humidity which was calculated from  specific humidity.
… Re-analysis data was taken from 1948-1958 and repeated 10-times and from the land  cell with the smallest Euclidean distance from the Poblet nature reserve …

datasets.name: Poblet nature reserve site characterization (Prades Mountains, NE Iberian Peninsula)

evidence.snippet: … Poblet nature reserve (Prades Mountains, Northeastern Iberian  Peninsula, 41° 19′ 58.05″ N, 1° 0′ 52.26″ E). The climate there is Mediterranean, with a mean annual  rainfall of 664 mm … and moderately warm temperatures (11.3 °C on average …)

inputs: Initial tree allometry and carbon pools (basal diameter, leaf area/LAI, root/shoot fractions, NSC concentrations)
Soil profile and boundary conditions for Richards equation (free drainage or groundwater Dirichlet condition)
Meteorological forcing time series from reanalysis (Tair, Tsoil, RH, precipitation, wind, insolation)
Treatment modifiers (precipitation scaling; groundwater-table depth; overstory canopy shading; elevated CO2; pruning multipliers and parameter changes)
outputs: Time series of biomass partitioning metrics (LMF, SMF, RMF) vs total dry mass
Root distribution / rooting depth metrics (e.g., Z95%, Z99.5%)
Recovery trajectories after pruning (shoot-root ratio, total biomass, productivity)
steps: Run a control simulation for 100 years at 6-hour time steps using NCEP/NCAR Reanalysis 1 forcing; repeat 1948–1958 forcing 10 times.

Initialize site/soil: Poblet-reserve-like soil/geology; solve Richards equation over a 30 m ‘soil column’ with a free-drainage lower boundary (control), and initialize soil-water potentials in hydrostatic equilibrium with a deep water table.

Initialize tree: start from a specified basal diameter and derived height/canopy width; set initial LAI; compute initial pool sizes using assumed carbon content fractions; set initial root distribution with an asymptotic depth function; set initial NSC concentrations by organ.
After ~2.6-year spin-up for allometry and soil hydrology equilibration, report biomass fractions and apply treatments as specified below.
Drought treatments: reduce precipitation to 50% and 75% of control; compare partitioning, rooting depth, and Huber value vs control as functions of total biomass.

Groundwater-access treatment: set a shallow groundwater table (2 m) with precipitation reduced to 50%; replace free-drainage boundary by a constant-pressure (Dirichlet) boundary and solve Richards equation only above the groundwater depth; track true water-table depth over time.
Competition (light) treatment: introduce an overstory canopy (30 m tall, LAI 3, K_n = 3.2×10^-2 m^-1) after spin-up to shade the target tree.
Elevated CO2 treatment: increase atmospheric CO2 to 600 ppm (no nutrient limitation represented).

Pruning recovery treatments: after (i) ~2.6 years and (ii) 30 years, remove 50% of either (a) all root-layer pools (both intralayer and interlayer) or (b) leaf + aboveground sapwood + heartwood pools; for shoot pruning also adjust parameters to reflect halved stem conductance and geometry (b2 halved, ξ halved, canopy area halved via b1×√0.5), then continue the remainder of the 100-year simulation.

evidence.snippet: … 100-years  at 6-hour time-steps with NOAA re-analysis data …
… Biomass fractions (LMF, SMF, RMF) are reported after a ~2.6 years spin up period for allometry and soil  hydrology to equilibrate.
… precipitation rates  were reduced to 50% and 75% of their control values.
… a shallow, 2 m deep water table … precipitation rates were reduced to 50% … replaced by a constant pressure (Dirichlet) boundary condition …
… a 30 m tall overstory with a LAI of 3 was  introduced …
… CO2 concentrations were elevated to 600 ppm …
… removing half of either its root or shoot mass … four total recovery experiments …

forcing.repetition: Repeated 10 times; nearest land grid cell to Poblet site
forcing.notes: Period chosen to avoid long-term warming/drying trends; RH computed from specific humidity.

site_soil.site: Poblet nature reserve (Prades Mountains, NE Iberian Peninsula)
site_soil.water_table_context: Water table estimated 70–100 m deep; initialization uses hydrostatic equilibrium with a 74 m deep water table.

initial_tree.root_depth_distribution_notes: Root carbon distributed by an asymptotic depth function Y = 1 - βd (notation as extracted); β chosen so 99% of root biomass is above 3 m depth.
initial_tree.NSC_initialization: NSC concentrations: 20% in leaves, 4% in wood; 20% of total NSC belowground.

evidence.snippet: … 6-hour time-steps with NOAA re-analysis data (NCEP/NCAR Reanalysis 1; Kalnay et al., 1996) … Re-analysis data was taken from 1948-1958 and repeated 10-times …
… Poblet nature reserve … 41° 19′ 58.05″ N, 1° 0′ 52.26″ E). The climate there is Mediterranean, with a mean annual  rainfall of 664 mm … (11.3 °C on average …)
… the lower  boundary condition … (at the bottom of the simulated soil column, which is here 30 m deep) … in free drainage … water table … 70-100 m …
… The initial allometry was calculated for a tree with a 1.5 · 10-2 m basal diameter … (~4.4 m and ~0.6 m, respectively). … initial LAI of 0.4 … initial RMF of 30% …
… Soil water potentials were initialized assuming hydrostatic equilibrium with a 74 m  deep water table …
… The extinction coefficient, β, was chosen so that 99%  of root biomass was above a 3 m depth …
… The NSC pool was initialized assuming NSC concentrations of  20% in leaves, 4% in wood, and that 20% of the total NSC is belowground …
… Biomass fractions … are reported after a ~2.6 years spin up period …

experiments.name: Reduced precipitation to 50% of control
experiments.comparison_basis: Compare allocation vs control as a function of total biomass (growth rates differ).

evidence.snippet: … conditions were the same as control experiment except precipitation rates  were reduced to 50% and 75% of their control values. …

experiments.name: Reduced precipitation to 75% of control
experiments.comparison_basis: Compare allocation vs control as a function of total biomass (growth rates differ).

evidence.snippet: … conditions were the same as control experiment except precipitation rates  were reduced to 50% and 75% of their control values. …

experiments.name: Reduced precipitation (50%) with shallow groundwater table

modifications.condition: constant pressure (Dirichlet) at bottom of Richards domain above groundwater

notes: Richards equation solved only for nodes above defined groundwater depth; nodes below assumed hydrostatic equilibrium.
Root growth constrained to layers where Ψsoil,i < 0 (so positive potentials below groundwater do not alter allometry under this constraint).
True water-table depth may vary seasonally and deepen over time; tracked and mean values reported.

evidence.snippet: … A third water stress experiment was performed with a shallow, 2 m deep water table … precipitation rates were reduced to 50% … replaced by a constant pressure (Dirichlet) boundary condition …

experiments.name: Aboveground competition (shading) via overstory canopy
experiments.notes: Represents an end-member case where the target tree is more light-stressed but belowground conditions are unchanged; overstory transpiration effects on soil are not represented.

evidence.snippet: … except a 30 m tall overstory with a LAI of 3 was  introduced … (Κn = 3.2 ∙ 10-2 m-1 …) after the ~2.6 spin-up  period.

experiments.name: Elevated atmospheric CO2
experiments.notes: Nutrient acquisition/limitation not represented in current THORP version; treatment represents elevated CO2 without nutrient limitation.

evidence.snippet: … identical to  the control except CO2 concentrations were elevated to 600 ppm … does not  describe nutrient acquisition and partitioning …

… two  in which pruning occurred after the initial ~2.6 year spin up period … We ‘pruned’ roots by simply reducing the root carbon pool of each soil layer by 50% (both  interlayer and intralayer pools halved) …

experiments.name: Recovery after early shoot pruning

changes.notes: Halve stem xylem conductance for a given basal diameter (semicircular trunk assumption).
Half as much woody biomass required to grow radially (maintain basal semicircularity).
Reduce canopy area by half to maintain LAI at pruning time.

notes: Text describes halving leaf and aboveground sapwood and heartwood carbon pools; sapwood/heartwood pools are aboveground in THORP’s carbon bookkeeping.

evidence.snippet: … we ‘pruned’ shoots by reducing the leaf and aboveground sap-  and heart-wood carbon pools by 50%. … b2  which controls stem conductance was halved … ξ  which governs wood volume was halved … b1 which controls canopy width  was multiplied by √0.5 …
… four total recovery experiments … two in which pruning occurred  after the first 30 years of simulation.

experiments.name: Recovery after late shoot pruning
notes: Text describes halving leaf and aboveground sapwood and heartwood carbon pools; sapwood/heartwood pools are aboveground in THORP’s carbon bookkeeping.

evidence.snippet: … four total recovery experiments … two in which pruning occurred  after the first 30 years of simulation.

additional_experiments.name: Groundwater-table depth sweep

modifications.schedule: Lower defined groundwater table in 2 m increments from 2 m to 20 m; then in 4 m increments to 40 m; then in 8 m increments to 80 m.

additional_experiments.purpose: Investigate role of soil moisture profiles on rooting depth and root distribution with depth.

evidence.snippet: … additional experiments were simulated with  deeper groundwater tables … Between experiments, the groundwater table was lowered in 2 m increments  down to 20 m, then down to 40 m in 4 m increments, and lastly down to 80 m in 8 m increments.

additional_experiments.notes: Described as additional experiments after the shallow groundwater-table run; the text indicates a series of runs across multiple groundwater depths.

limitations.text: The elevated-CO2 simulation does not include nutrient acquisition/limitation, so it represents an idealized response without nutrient constraints.

evidence.snippet: … does not  describe nutrient acquisition and partitioning, so this simulation represents the case of elevated CO2  without the possibility of nutrient limitation.

limitations.text: The shading/competition simulation does not include the overstory trees’ transpiration effects on soil moisture, so belowground resource acquisition for the target tree is not affected by the added canopy.

evidence.snippet: … In reality, additional trees from the overstory would also  transpire, dry soils … and thus directly  influence belowground allocation … as well.

limitations.text: The shoot-pruning disturbance is implemented as halving aboveground pools with additional parameter adjustments to satisfy THORP’s assumptions; it does not strictly represent literal pruning.

evidence.snippet: … Our disturbance does not strictly represent  true pruning like in the Brouwer (1962) experiment … Additionally, ‘pruning’ shoots required extra changes so not to  invalidate THORP’s assumptions …

chunk_plan.title: S.10 Simulation Descriptions
chunk_plan.rationale: Defines the experimental protocols, forcing data, initialization, and treatment perturbations used to evaluate THORP across control, drought/groundwater, shading, elevated CO2, and pruning recovery simulations.

chunk_plan.title: References
chunk_plan.rationale: Bibliography; generally non-model content (useful only if citation mapping is required).
